entry.title: Announcing SAP Inside Track Hyderabad: Join Us on July 26, 2025!
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce that SAP Inside Track Hyderabad is back and better than ever! Mark your calendars for July 26, 2025, as we bring together SAP enthusiasts, experts, and professionals for a day of insightful sessions, networking, and innovation. Registration is now open, and we invite you to secure your spot for this exciting event.

What is SAP Inside Track?   
SAP Inside Track is a community-driven event where SAP professionals, developers, consultants, architects, and leaders come together to share their knowledge and insights on various SAP-related topics. This grassroots initiative, organized by members of the SAP Community, provides a platform for fostering connections, staying updated on the latest SAP technologies, and gaining recognition for your technical expertise. 
Know more about SAP Inside Track Here!

Event Highlights

  1. Expert-Led Sessions
    • Keynote Speeches: Hear from industry leaders and SAP experts about the latest advancements in SAP technologies.
    • Technical Deep Dives: Participate in sessions that cover a wide range of topics, from SAP S/4HANA to SAP Cloud Platform, and everything in between.
    • Hands-On Workshops: Engage in interactive workshops designed to provide practical experience with SAP tools and solutions.
  2. Networking Opportunities
    • Meet the Experts: Connect with SAP professionals, developers, and consultants from various industries.
    • Community Building: Build relationships with peers who share your passion for SAP and technology.

Why Attend?

  • Expand Your Knowledge: Gain insights into the latest SAP technologies and trends.
  • Enhance Your Skills: Participate in hands-on workshops and technical sessions.
  • Grow Your Network: Connect with like-minded professionals and industry experts.
  • Get Inspired: Discover new ideas and innovative solutions that can transform your business.

Registration Details

Registration for SAP Inside Track Hyderabad in Open! Don’t miss this opportunity to be part of a vibrant community and stay ahead in the ever-evolving world of SAP. 

Visit the Registration Page: SAP Inside Track 2025

Note: This is an in-person event only.

Event Details

  • Date: July 26, 2025
  • Time: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM
  • Venue: SAP Callidus Cloud, 6th floor, Building No. 12-B, Raheja Mindspace IT Park (Google Maps)
  • Contact: For any queries, please contact us at sapcommunityhyderabad@gmail.com

Speaker Registration: 

Speaker registrations are now open! If you're an expert in building AI applications on SAP BTP, developing innovative Business AI solutions, or working with embedded AI in SAP solutions like SAP S/4HANA or SuccessFactors, we welcome your contribution. We also invite partners and customers to showcase their work.   Link to Submit Speaker Nomination 

Exclusive Sponsorship Opportunities: 

With over 7500 community members across locations in the past 12 months, we’re eager to continue growing. If you're interested in sponsoring the event, please comment below, and we’ll reach out to you. This year, we have exclusive sponsor bundles that offer opportunities to showcase your content and expertise across multiple locations, including Bangalore, Gurgaon, Hyderabad, and Pune. 

Join the Conversation

Stay connected and join the conversation on social media using the hashtag #sitHYD. Follow us on Twitter and LinkedIn, for the latest updates and announcements.

We look forward to seeing you at SAP Inside Track Hyderabad 2025! Let’s come together to learn, share, and innovate.

 

Thanks & Regards

SAP Community HYD

entry.title: Call for Speakers: SAP Inside Track Hyderabad 2025, July 26 2025
entry.content:
Event Overview

Date: July 26, 2025
Location: Hyderabad, India
Venue: SAP Callidus Cloud, 6th floor, Building No. 12-B, Raheja Mindspace IT Park (Google Maps)

SAP Inside Track Hyderabad is a community-driven event that brings together SAP users, developers, and experts from various industries. The event aims to foster learning, networking, and collaboration through a series of insightful sessions, workshops, and discussions.

Call for Speakers

We are looking for dynamic speakers to present on a variety of topics related to SAP technologies and solutions, we want to hear from you!

Topics of Interest

  1. Business AI Showcases & Use Cases
  2. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)
  3. Embedded AI across Solutions - SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors & Other SAP Products
  4. SAP S/4HANA, SuccessFactors & Other SAP Products

Why Speak at SAP Inside Track Hyderabad?

  • Share Your Expertise: Showcase your knowledge and insights to a diverse and engaged audience.
  • Network with Peers: Connect with other SAP professionals, thought leaders, and industry experts.
  • Enhance Your Profile: Gain recognition and visibility within the SAP community.
  • Contribute to the Community: Play a part in the growth and development of the SAP ecosystem.

 How to register

To submit your proposal, please visit this link and complete the online submission form. If you have any questions or need further information, please contact us at sapcommunityhyderabad@gmail.com.

Next Steps: Once you submit your proposal,
Step 1: Our expert team will go through your proposal and if it is shortlisted, we will contact your for more details.
Step 2: This will again be discussed internally with the experts and once shortlisted, we will inform you about the next steps – like presentation format, social media advertisement etc.,

 

Thanks & Regards

SAP Community Hyd

entry.title: How to Use Standard SAP Tables (BKPF, BSEG) in CDS Views with ABAP Cloud Programming on SAP BTP?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently learning SAP BTP and working with SAP ABAP Cloud Programming for the first time in Eclipse. While creating a CDS View, I noticed that I couldn’t use standard SAP tables like BKPF or BSEG directly in the CDS View.

During my research on how to use standard SAP tables in ABAP Cloud Programming, I found that these tables need to be released first before they can be used in the cloud environment.

Could someone please explain how to release these tables for use in the cloud? Or is there another recommended way to access standard table data in SAP BTP / ABAP Cloud?

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: Re: Standalone Retrofit SAP Solution Manager 7.2 - Configuration
entry.content:
Hello Rishi,

We are trying to retrofit 2 different domains using different transport paths, do we need to go with standalone or cross-release retrofit?

Regards,

Sandeep

entry.title: Re: How to hide post button of "Create Supplier Invoice"
entry.content:
Hi Bob,

I am looking for a similar setting option in the area of posting supplier invoices (created out of SAP Central Invoice Management). In this process, too, there is a requirement that the “Post” button should explicitly not be used and instead the “Parked as completed” button should be used to trigger the supplier invoice workflow before posting.

Best regards
Michael

entry.title: Re: How to add Odata Service to a Fiori App?
entry.content:
It's unclear if you service is a OData V2 or an OData V4

/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE -> only used in V2

/IWBEP/V4_ADMIN + /IWFND/V4_ADMIN -> only used in V4

Regardless how you created the service - it must be registered/published in one of those 2 depending the OData version.

Please clarify:

  • OData version
  • If it's already registered in the right transaction
  • If your system does support HTTPS with certificate or not
  • If you are able to call the service using /IWFND/GW_CLIENT in HTTP and/or HTTPS

I suggest to not use the "Connect to an OData Service" in VS Code/BAS. Use option "Connect to a system" instead. (once again this bad naming confusing people). Like that you will input system and port information and the wizard will show you the services already registered (if this is not working it means you don't have basic services/setup done in this environment yet).

This option will change the way ui5.yaml are configured.  If the issue persisit please share the ui5.yaml file and the /IWFND/ERROR_LOG details here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori tools May 2024 release adds closer integration with ABAP Development Tools
entry.content:
hi @Brenomatias 

Based on the screenshot, I am assuming you are working on BAS. It looks like there may be an auth issue with the destination [s4h]. In order for this flow to succeed, the destination must be able to connect to a chosen service and retrieve metadata. 

Can you please check the service via the service center, see documentation here. You should be able to click 'SAP System' and then your [s4h] destination, and the desired service should present with a green symbol when clicked, and the Create Project button should be enabled on the service details page.

If this is not the case, and you are encountering auth issues at this point, please refer to some of our guided answers #1 and #2  . If these do not help, please provide more information of your current set up.

Best regards,

Cian

Fiori tools team

entry.title: Re: REST API Integration and Data Visualization in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: Thank you, Spandhana, for sharing this insightful blog. The information provided is highly valuable and much appreciated.

entry.title: Re: How to Use Standard SAP Tables (BKPF, BSEG) in CDS Vi...
entry.content:
Dear Shivam 

Only SAP can release standard tables or objects. As a developer, you can only use what has already been released.

 

Have you considered using released CDS views or APIs instead of accessing tables directly?

Steps to find released alternatives:

a) Search for released CDS views in ADT (Eclipse)
Use Ctrl+Shift+A (Open ABAP Object) and search for:

I_BKPF*
I_BSEG*
I_AccountingDocument*
I_JournalEntry*

b) Check the Released Objects in your ABAP Cloud project
In Eclipse:

  • Right-click your project → ABAP Cloud Environment → Released Objects
  • Filter by object type (e.g., CDS View)

c) Browse SAP API Business Hub
Go to: https://api.sap.com
Search for APIs like:

  • Journal Entry – Read
  • Read Accounting Document
  • I_GLAccountLineItem

Examples of useful released CDS views:

  • I_JournalEntry
  • I_GLAccountLineItem
  • I_AccountingDocument
  • I_Customer
  • I_Supplieŕ’

Tip

If you can't find a direct replacement for a table like BKPF or BSEG:

  • Check if you can join multiple released views
  • Use custom RAP projection views
  • Consider consuming data via ODATA APIs or SAP Graph
  • Follow clean-core architecture principles

Best regards

entry.title: Issue with deleted Purchase Requisition still generating MRP demand (Fiori App F4795)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am facing a situation related to purchase requisitions created by MRP and deleted via the Fiori App F4795 — Hand Over Purchase Requisitions (F4795).

Scenario:

A purchase requisition generated by MRP for the parent item was deleted via the Fiori App F4795 Hand Over Purchase Requisitions (Table EBAN, field LOEKZ = X).

However, the MRP still shows a Subassembly Requirement (BB) for the child item, with the same quantity and date as the deleted requisition.

The parent component (semi-finished product) still has a reservation that was not cleared (Table RESB, field KZEAR ≠ X).

  • Has anyone faced similar behavior, where a deleted requisition still generates MRP demand due to an open Subassembly Requirement or an uncleared reservation?
  • Is there any official solution or correction note for this?
  • What is the best practice to ensure that deleted requisitions do not continue generating demand in MRP when using F4795?

I have read the article by @Caetano about this app and would appreciate any help with this difficulty.

Thank you very much in advance for your support!

entry.title: Not able to find the business catalog SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_GLO_GCO_PC
entry.content:
Unable to find the SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_GLO_GCO_PC business catalog for Activate Document and Reporting Compliance Features.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Not able to find the business catalog SAP_CA_BC_IC_LN...
entry.content:
Hi all,

 

Kindly make sure you have activated 5XU scope item in S/4Hana Cloud system. Once activated the 5XU scope item, assign the business catalog SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_GLO_GCO_PC to your business user for 'Activate Document and Reporting Compliance Features'.

 

Please refer the 3237813 - DRC - Run Statutory Reports (F1515) - Analytics icon is not visible to user KBA for more information.

 

Best regards,

Atanu

SAP Support - S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: SAP Datasphere Analytical Model Performance Trace & PlanViz
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to analyze the Performance of an Analytical Model in SAP Datasphere to identify possible design issues. Therefore I follow the SAP Help documentation to extract the MDS Statement and generate the PlanViz File.

But I currently have the following problem:

  1. I execute the Model in SAC and retreive the MDS statement. Here I have a runtime of about ~40 seconds.
  2. Then I call generate the MDS statement with the CALL SYS.EXECUTE_MDS(...) statement. Here I have the behaviour that this is then generated in around ~1 second and the PlanViz is not reflecting the ~40 seconds runtime in the frontend.

My current assumption is that this is due to the MDS cache. But in the Database Console also cannot flush it, due to missing authorization. 

Does anybody has had similar issues or knows any best practices to generate a correct PlanViz out of an Analytical Model MDS statement?

Thank you for your input!

Regards

Christian

entry.title: Re: Credit checks available in SAP Credit Management (FSCM)
entry.content:
Hello @Edina , 

Thanks for the detailed explanation above. This is really helpful. 

I have a question regarding a Custom Check Rule. 
Let's say we utilise BADI UKM_CHECK_STEP to write the logic for customer specific needs, but then, we want the parameters within that logic to be made configurable in the Check Rule. 
How can we achieve that? Could you please help with that?

For example: in the standard check step: 020 Check for Maximum Document Value, we can assign the Maximum Doc Value per Credit Segment, when we configure the check rule. 


Similarly, if we want certain fields that are not delivered by SAP standard, how can we bring those fields and make them a configurable field?
Thanking you in anticipation.

entry.title: I want to mass delete some old Purchase requistions but MASS not working
entry.content: I have tried using MASS to delete very old purchase requistions in SAP the MASS update says successful but it doesnt work. If i go back into MASS for the relevant PR i used for testing its unticked and also in table EBAN its not ticked. I have tried using ME57 but the closed option is greyed out. How do I get rid of all these old PR's there are hundreds of them. I can find nothing on this or any other community. Thanks Dean

entry.title: Re: 公有云系统中，量差与价差的核算未能分别体现，全部核算到价差中，没有体现量差，如何处置？
entry.content:
Hi @lemon-xue ,

您好，您试过“物料价格分析”（Material Price Analysis CKM3）app里面的“评估数量结构（Valuated Quantity Structure）”按钮吗？位于屏幕上方菜单第3个左右图标。它会在屏幕左侧以树状结构显示所输入产成品的各生产级别原料及活动类型的实际耗用数量，初次评估价值、实际价值及两者差异。

When choosing Valuated Quantity Structure icon, you see the a tree view showing production structure for your specified material and all of its production levels, including finished goods, semi-finished goods, raw materials, and activity types. For each of them, actual consumed quantities, preliminary valuation, actual values, and differences are displayed.

Let me know if it answers your question!

Thanks!

Grace

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP integration suite giving 403 error while triggers data from SAP
entry.content: Another top tip: after doing this, it can happen that the system needs 1-2h to apply these changes!! I got HTTP 403 for more than 60min after this configuration, before it was applied and working.

entry.title: Re: Automation for cheque bounce process in SAP public cloud
entry.content:
Hello @Mithundogra 

S/4HANA Public cloud does not provide a standard API to reset cleared customer line items (e.g., in bounced cheque scenarios). While reversal of posted documents is supported via the A_CustomerPaymentDocument API, clearing must still be reset manually.

To manage high volumes (such as 75–100 bounced cheques daily), SAP recommends using SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) to simulate the necessary Fiori actions, such as:

  • Accessing the Manage Customer Line Items app
  • Resetting cleared items
  • Then triggering document reversal via API

This is currently the most efficient and supported approach in Public Cloud. A native API for clearing reset has not yet been released or scheduled in future public cloud roadmaps.

Thank you and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: SAP Change Management with Azure DevOps: Transforming Enterprise Operations
entry.content:
This blog post was jointly created by @Bharath_Sanmani and @Holger-Bruchelt 

Introduction

At Microsoft, we’re one of the largest SAP customers in the world. For years, we've combined SAP's robust capabilities with a wide array of non-SAP tools to optimize our development and operational processes. While SAP’s transport mechanism is well-established and reliable, it was originally designed for more traditional, linear development cycles.

In contrast, today’s digital enterprises thrive on agility. Modern development methodologies demand faster iteration, better collaboration, seamless integration, and real-time visibility — areas where traditional SAP tooling can fall short.

Historically, we relied on third-party tools for SAP change and release management. These tools served us for a while, but over time their limitations became apparent. In Fiscal Year 2020 alone, we saw nearly 450 support tickets raised due to tool instability and usability issues. With the platform approaching end-of-life and growing dissatisfaction among users, it was clear that a change was needed.

Moreover, the lack of integration with key platforms such as Azure DevOps, Compliance Security Questionnaire, and Virtual Forge created manual overhead and fragmented workflows. Performance decline in release cycles began to impact our ability to deliver with speed and confidence — putting our commitment to 100% on-time deployments at risk.

 

The Ideation: Why Azure DevOps for SAP?

Azure DevOps (ADO) is a cloud-based platform that enables agile project management, version control, CI/CD, and collaborative software development. Across Microsoft, our non-SAP teams were already reaping the benefits of Azure DevOps throughout the entire software development lifecycle. In contrast, SAP teams were using ADO only for planning, while the actual transport and deployment remained siloed.

That’s when the lightbulb moment occurred:
Why not extend Azure DevOps into the SAP landscape?

We envisioned a single, unified toolchain — where SAP developers could not only plan and track changes but also create, manage, and propagate transports directly from Azure DevOps. This would ensure a consistent experience across all engineering teams, increase transparency, and accelerate delivery.

Bringing the Vision to Life

This blog post marks the beginning of a series where we share our journey of transforming SAP change management by integrating it with Azure DevOps. In this initial post, we've set the stage by describing the challenges, motivations, and the opportunity we identified.

What we have built at Microsoft today is a production-ready internal solution that automates and governs SAP transports via Azure DevOps — bringing SAP change management into the modern DevOps era. It’s already driving higher productivity, greater control, and a more seamless user experience for our internal teams.

Though this solution is currently tailored for our internal use, we believe it offers a blueprint for other SAP customers looking to modernize their development and deployment practices.

Let’s bring SAP into the DevOps era — together.
We’d love to hear your thoughts!

 

Why DevOps for SAP?

As Forrester puts it:

"DevOps accelerates service delivery, drives quality, and — most importantly — enhances customer experiences."

SAP environments, traditionally seen as rigid, can benefit immensely from DevOps practices — achieving faster delivery, better quality, and more agility.

Motivation

The partnership between SAP and Microsoft simplifies customers' journey to RISE with SAP on Microsoft Azure. As customers modernize their SAP workloads, integrating Azure DevOps becomes a natural evolution.

Key insights:

  • The global DevOps market (USD 8B in 2022) is set to grow at 20% CAGR till 2032.
  • DevOps for SAP remains an untapped opportunity — with few mature solutions.
  • The DevOps community believes SAP needs a different approach compared to traditional DevOps pipelines.
  • Thousands of customers are running SAP with RISE on Azure, even more use SAP and Microsoft solutions (like Microsoft 365). For customers already on Azure this means that they can even get Azure DevOps for free to get started!

SAP at Microsoft

SAP on Azure: A Huge Opportunity

Many enterprises are moving their SAP workloads to Azure for scalability, security, and resilience. Extending Azure DevOps into this landscape (whether it is RISE with SAP, native or a hybrid setup) is a powerful differentiator — offering:

  • Unified tooling
  • Improved compliance
  • Integrated pipelines for SAP and non-SAP systems

SAP at Microsoft: Our Own Transformation Story

The Challenges

Microsoft’s internal Commerce + Ecosystems (once relied on 3rd Party tool for SAP change management.
Issues faced:

  • High ticket volumes on tool issues
  • Poor stability and usability
  • Lack of integration with ADO, Compliance, and Virtual Forge tools.
  • Manual, fragmented processes slowing down releases
  • Threat to enterprise agility and on-time deployments

The Solution

In response, the SAP Engineering Excellence team decided to replace the 3rd party tool with Azure DevOps — a native Microsoft platform.

This shift delivered a fully integrated SAP change management solution within Azure DevOps.

 

 

 ADO Change Request: 

 

 
 

 

Getting code out of SAP - abapGit

One of the key requirements was to make the developed ABAP code more easily available in our sandbox and other SAP systems. abapGit provided the perfect way to work with ABAP code outside of an SAP system and was the starting point for a full transformation.

 
Extending Azure DevOps for SAP: Custom Features
To tailor ADO for SAP, our team added key features:
  • SAP-specific work items (e.g., SAP Change Request)
  • ADO pull requests for SAP code review
  • Chatbot integration for user interactions
  • AI-powered code review
  • Mandated test evidence attachment
  • Automated code comparison
  • Automated Client Open & Closing
  • Simplified approvals (adaptive cards in Outlook)
  • Workflow time reduction
  • Transport request optimization
  • Elimination of 3rd-party license costs
  • Centralized dashboards for reporting

Benefits of SAP Change Management with Azure DevOps

✅Single-tool experience for SAP and non-SAP teams
✅Standardized change requests, approvals, and transports
✅Enhanced security with built-in code analysis and compliance checks
✅Accelerated deployment through CI/CD pipelines
✅Real-time monitoring, alerts, and feedback loops

The Impact: Real Results at Microsoft

  • 12,000+ SAP Change Requests deployed to Production
  • 26 landscapes and 90 Feature Crews onboarded to ADO
  • 50% increase in developer efficiency in overall Change Management
  • 90% reduction in tool-related incidents
  • User satisfaction score of 4.5/5
  • Ready to be used in RISE and non-RISE scenarios

What are our Users Saying?

Who Benefits the Most?

 
Conclusion
 
SAP Change Management with Azure DevOps is not just a productivity upgrade — it’s a game changer.
Microsoft’s own success story proves the massive potential.
 
The opportunity is clear:
✅For enterprises modernizing SAP
✅For Microsoft’s DevOps ecosystem
✅For customers seeking faster, more reliable innovation

It’s time to reimagine SAP Change Management — the DevOps way.

entry.title: Re: IDOC Error message "A company code cannot be determined for LI XXXXXXXXXX"
entry.content: Did anyone able to resolve it? Please suggest, facing the same issue

entry.title: How to export data to excel by code?
entry.content:
Hello everyone, I'm developer but I am having a problem with my code that is I want to export data to excel without using OLE but I want to export data in external format when using CALL FUNCTION CONVERT DATE TO INTERNAL when the data displayed on ALV is DD/MM/YYYY then I want when exporting data to excel it will display as YYYYMMDD. I need help.
ABAP Development  SAP BTP ABAP environment 

FORM download_data_to_excel USING p_table TYPE tabname.

  DATA: lt_data      TYPE REF TO data,

        lt_fields    TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF dfies,

        ls_field     TYPE dfies,

        lv_localpath TYPE string,

        lv_msg       TYPE string,

        lo_alv       TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table,

        lv_xstring   TYPE xstring,

        lt_solix     TYPE solix_tab,

        lv_bytecount TYPE i.

 

  FIELD-SYMBOLS: <lt_table> TYPE STANDARD TABLE.



  " Lấy metadata bảng

  CALL FUNCTION 'DDIF_FIELDINFO_GET'

    EXPORTING

      tabname   = p_table

      langu     = sy-langu

    TABLES

      dfies_tab = lt_fields

    EXCEPTIONS

      not_found = 1

      OTHERS    = 2.

  IF sy-subrc <> 0 OR lt_fields IS INITIAL.

    MESSAGE '❌ Không thể lấy metadata của bảng.' TYPE 'E'.

  ENDIF.

 

  " Tạo bảng động chứa dữ liệu

  CREATE DATA lt_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF (p_table).

  ASSIGN lt_data->* TO <lt_table>.



  " Lấy toàn bộ dữ liệu từ bảng

  SELECT * FROM (p_table) INTO TABLE @<lt_table>.

  IF sy-subrc <> 0 OR lines( <lt_table> ) = 0.

    MESSAGE 'ℹ️ Bảng không có dữ liệu để xuất.' TYPE 'I'.

    RETURN.

  ENDIF.

 

  " Hộp thoại chọn nơi lưu file Excel

  CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>file_save_dialog

    EXPORTING

      default_extension   = 'xlsx'

      default_file_name   = |Data_{ p_table }_{ sy-datum }_{ sy-uzeit }.xlsx|

      initial_directory   = 'C:\TEMP\'

      prompt_on_overwrite = 'X'

    CHANGING

      filename            = lv_localpath

      path                = lv_msg

      fullpath            = lv_localpath

    EXCEPTIONS

      cntl_error          = 1

      error_no_gui        = 2

      OTHERS              = 3.

  IF sy-subrc <> 0 OR lv_localpath IS INITIAL.

    MESSAGE 'ℹ️ Bạn chưa chọn nơi lưu.' TYPE 'I'.

    RETURN.

  ENDIF.

 

  " Tạo ALV từ dữ liệu

  TRY.

      cl_salv_table=>factory(

        IMPORTING

          r_salv_table = lo_alv

        CHANGING

          t_table      = <lt_table> ).

 

      " Đặt tiêu đề cột đơn giản

      DATA(lo_columns) = lo_alv->get_columns( ).

      lo_columns->set_optimize( abap_true ).

 

      " Chuyển đổi sang XLSX

      lo_alv->to_xml(

        EXPORTING

          xml_type    = if_salv_bs_xml=>c_type_xlsx

        RECEIVING

          xml         = lv_xstring

      ).

 

      " Chuyển XSTRING sang SOLIX

      CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY'

        EXPORTING

          buffer        = lv_xstring

        IMPORTING

          output_length = lv_bytecount

        TABLES

          binary_tab    = lt_solix.

 

      " Tải file xuống

      CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>gui_download

        EXPORTING

          bin_filesize            = lv_bytecount

          filename                = lv_localpath

          filetype                = 'BIN'

        CHANGING

          data_tab                = lt_solix

        EXCEPTIONS

          file_write_error        = 1

          no_batch                = 2

          gui_refuse_filetransfer = 3

          invalid_type            = 4

          no_authority            = 5

          unknown_error           = 6

          header_not_allowed      = 7

          separator_not_allowed   = 8

          filesize_not_allowed    = 9

          header_too_long         = 10

          dp_error_create         = 11

          dp_error_send           = 12

          dp_error_write          = 13

          unknown_dp_error        = 14

          access_denied           = 15

          dp_out_of_memory        = 16

          disk_full               = 17

          dp_timeout              = 18

          file_not_found          = 19

          dataprovider_exception  = 20

          control_flush_error     = 21

          not_supported_by_gui    = 22

          error_no_gui            = 23

          OTHERS                  = 24.

      IF sy-subrc <> 0.

        MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno

                WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.

      ELSE.

        MESSAGE |✅ Xuất dữ liệu thành công. Tổng cộng { lines( <lt_table> ) } dòng.| TYPE 'S'.

      ENDIF.

 

    CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_error).

      MESSAGE |❌ Lỗi khi xuất file Excel: { lx_error->get_text( ) }| TYPE 'E'.

  ENDTRY.

ENDFORM.












LOOP AT lt_fields INTO ls_field.

ASSIGN COMPONENT ls_field-fieldname OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO <lv_value>.

IF sy-subrc = 0 AND <lv_value> IS NOT INITIAL.

CALL METHOD OF lo_sheet 'Cells' = lo_cell EXPORTING #1 = lv_row #2 = lv_col.

 

IF ls field-datatype = 'DATS'

CALL FUNCTION CONVERT DATE TO INTERNAL

EXPORTING

Date_external = <lv_value>

Accept_initial_date = abap_on

IMPORTING

Date_internal = lv_data

EXCEPTIONS

Date_external_is_invalid = 1

OTHERS = 2.

IF sy-subrc <> 0.

* Implement suitable error handling here

ENDIF.

SET PROPERTY OF lo_cell 'Value' = lv_data.

ELSEIF ls_field-datatype = 'TIMS'.

 

WRITE <lv_value> TO lv_data2.

REPLACE ALL OCCURRENCES OF ':' IN lv_data2 WITH ' ‘.

SET PROPERTY OF lo_cell 'Value' = lv_data2.

ELSE.

 

SET PROPERTY OF lo_cell 'Value' = <lv_value>.  “20250610

SET PROPERTY OF lo cell 'NumberFormat' = ‘@’.

ENDIF.

Rushi

entry.title: Re: RAP unmannaged scenario - Create Sales Order with EML
entry.content:
I’m working on the exact same scenario — RAP unmanaged with draft and finalize. I managed to get the sales order number in the save method by calling a handler class and using the Convert Key method.

However, I ran into an issue: when creating multiple sales orders at once, the Convert Key method only returns one sales order number.

My question is — can we use I_SalesOrderTP to create multiple sales orders in a single CREATE call? Or is it designed for single-entity creation only?

entry.title: Re: Print GS1-128 Bar code using adobe forms
entry.content: sorry.. the barcode object Code128SSCC is only suitable for die SSCC number. You can use the barcode object CODE128, CODE128C, or CODE128A for variable length / alphanumeric . Just build the barcode without any parenthesis around the AI's and put a [F1] at the beginning. The [F1] marks the Code128 as a GS1-128 for the barcodereader. Example coding AI's 15, 3302, 10: [F1]151603003302079300100331065.

entry.title: SAP S4/HANA Cloud Public J78 Cash Flow reconciliation
entry.content:
Hello,

I am testing SAP S4/HANA Cloud Public J78 test script: Cash Flow reconciliation
The app: Reconcile cash flow shows no data, The reconciliation status of Bank Account columns shows: 
NOT APPLICABLE.
There are open supplier, customer items, and also banks statements in the system.
All the preliminary steps are activated.
In reconcile cash flow statement app the reconciliation status of Bank Account columns should be on UNRECONCILED mode to be able to perform the CF reconciliation.
I have also checked the cash flow reconciliation job, it doesn't work either.
I enclose a picture of it.

Could you please help me how to fix this issue step by step?
Thank you.
Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Issue with deleted Purchase Requisition still generat...
entry.content:
Hello

Make sure that you have the following note implemented:

3214135 - Hand Over Purchase Requisitions - Deletion of belonging Subcontractor Requirement does not work

If you have inconsistent subcontracting requirements, you can find instructions to fix them in the following WIKI:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/mrp/3138697854.html

Best regards,
Caetano

HI @elemaugustine 
Welcome - Nice to meet you.
I want to encourage your to  ask a question in the Q&A area.
This makes it easier  to follow-up:
That way other learners can benefit from the answer:
If you ask the question there other learners  can search for it in the future if they run into the same issue and there we have more features available for responses.
Would l you mind to repost ist?
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: How to reuse access control from a released SAP CDS entity in ABAP Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi Andre,

This is a very interesting topic.

Should we always refer to I_BUSINESSUSERBASIC, even if you have built several layers above ZI_BUSINESSUSERBASIC?

For example:

  • ZI_BUSINESSUSER_L3 reads the data from ZI_BUSINESSUSER_L2
  • ZI_BUSINESSUSER_L2 reads the data from ZI_BUSINESSUSER_L1
  • ZI_BUSINESSUSER_L1 reads the data from ZI_BUSINESSUSERBASIC
  • ZI_BUSINESSUSERBASIC reads the data from I_BUSINESSUSERBASIC

For each CDS view we create an access where we use the inheriting option.

I assume that in the above scenario  “I_BUSINESSUSERBASIC” is the base entity?

Or should we refer to CDS-view from which we read?

For example:

  • ZI_BUSINESSUSER_L2 is the base entity in ZI_BUSINESSUSER_L3
  • ZI_BUSINESSUSER_L1 is the base entity in ZI_BUSINESSUSER_L2
  • ZI_BUSINESSUSER_BASIC is the base entity in ZI_BUSINESSUSER_L1
  • …

We noticed that if ZI_BUSINESSUSER_L2 has another key than I_BUSINESSUSERBASIC, we got different result in ZI_BUSINESSUSER_L3.

entry.title: Re: Automatic Billing Creation Through Sales Order Output...
entry.content:
Hi Vijay, 

You can achieve automatic billing creation through the output type mechanism in SD, triggered right after sales order creation.

 Here's a proven approach:

  1. Create a custom output type using T-code NACE  (e.g., for application V1 – Sales).

  2. Set the transmission medium to 6 (Function Module).

  3. BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT (to ensure the sales order is saved),

    • then BAPI_BILLINGDOC_CREATEMULTIPLE to generate the invoice.

  4. Handle the RETURN and SUCCESS tables to confirm creation and log errors.

Here is the sample Code  I  Used for Automatic Billing Invoice Creation 

*     Commit the Sales Document; Without Committing, The Billing Invoice Will not be Created.
      CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
        EXPORTING
          wait = 'X'.

      DATA: lt_billing_data TYPE TABLE OF bapivbrk,
            lt_return       TYPE TABLE OF bapireturn1,
            ls_billing_data TYPE bapivbrk,
            it_sucess       TYPE TABLE OF bapivbrksuccess.

*     Populate billing header data
      ls_billing_data-ref_doc = vbak-vbeln.
      ls_billing_data-ordbilltyp = vbak-auart.
      ls_billing_data-bill_date = vbak-erdat - 1.
      ls_billing_data-ref_item = vbap-posnr.
      ls_billing_data-ref_doc_ca  = 'C'.
      APPEND ls_billing_data TO lt_billing_data.

*     Create Individual Customer Billing Document
      CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_BILLINGDOC_CREATEMULTIPLE'
        TABLES
          billingdatain = lt_billing_data
          return        = lt_return
          success       = it_sucess.

      READ TABLE lt_return INTO DATA(ls_return) WITH KEY type = 'E'.
      IF sy-subrc <> 0.
        CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
          EXPORTING
            wait = 'X'.
      ENDIF.

This approach is safe and controlled because it uses standard SAP output processing logic, ensuring the billing happens only after successful order creation.

Best Regards,
Kasi Reddy M.

entry.title: Re: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide

entry.title: Re: Show only manager item-comments in employee signature
entry.content:
Hi @troman 

Indeed, the others-ratingtab-item-permission or setting up field-permission restrictions for item-comments will not allow you to determine the owner-role (only supported in section-permission). 

With the owner-role you can decide whose comment is visible to whom, but this is not available for the comments at the item level. 

There was an enhancement request a while ago about this requirement 

Owner Roles Feature for Item comments https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/254393

Regards, 

Jordi

entry.title: Re: Automatic Billing Creation Through Sales Order Output...
entry.content:
This is the Include Program - > MV45AFZZ 

This is the Exit you need to implement - > userexit_save_document

entry.title: Re: How Developer Extensibility lets you create your own Situation Handling Use Case (3/3)
entry.content:
Hi Jz,
a business user will see all situation instances for which he or she is responsible. this is independent from setting an instance to the assign to me state using the above described button in the My Situations - Extended app.
Which situation instances a user can see as

  • notification
  • entries in the My Situations app
  • tiles in the To-Dos section

and since recently though

  • Joule ( SAP Video - Joule, the AI Copilot in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition )

is configured through responsibility management
( Situation Handling: How to use Responsibility Management efficiently )

best,
Dr. Jörg Rett

entry.title: SAP Datasphere Delta flow with aggregation
entry.content:
Hi all, 

we want to replicate a BW flow that from Datasource 0FI_AR_04 in delta loads DSO A.
from this DSO A the data loads with a delta DTP in another DSO B where the key figures are in sum with a part of the keys of DSO A. Below are the tables for the example.
it is necessary to replicate this flow in datasphere. We have created the tables ZA(DSO A) and ZB(DSO B) both with delta capture.

now SAP DSPH The flow from datasource to table ZA, works correctly because the key figures are aggregated by overwriting, but now there is the problem from ZA to ZB since in the transformation Flow it does not seem to be possible to aggregate the key figures by sum and it does not even aggregate by key since it returns an error on the unique key (see example in the table), how can we manage this type of flow on DSPH? is there a standard procedure or best case to follow?

error for sum aggregation

 

 

 

 

example aggregation flow in BW:

in DSPH doesn't work the collect for a part of the same table key before loading the data from ZA to target(ZB):

 

 

thanks.

entry.title: Request for information: ANABIN integration in SAP
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are currently planning to integrate ANABIN with our SAP system. The main goal is to be able to find the equivalent of a candidate’s degree within our SAP processes. We are aware that there is already an existing implementation of ANABIN integration in SAP, and we would like to know how this integration was achieved.

In particular, we are interested in:

  • Which technical interfaces or methods were used for the integration?

  • Are there existing SAP solutions, modules, or best practices available for this use case?

  • What prerequisites are required for connecting ANABIN to SAP?

  • Is there any available documentation or example projects you can share?

  • How can we export all data from ANABIN at once or in bulk, in order to import or process it in SAP? Are there recommended approaches or tools for this?

We would greatly appreciate any guidance or reference materials, as we are aiming to implement a similar solution for finding degree equivalency.

Thank you very much for your support!

Best regards

entry.title: Automated Integration in Content agent to enable BTP services for transport
entry.content:
Automate Destination Creation

Excited to introduce the new feature from SAP Content Agent UI to automates the destination configuration for SAP BTP services that previously required manual efforts. These include:

  1. SAP Cloud Integration
  2. SAP API Management
  3. Role Collections
  4. SAP Cloud Transport Management service

Motivation

As a content developer working with various SAP BTP services, you often need to set up different configuration and it involves going through tedious documentation and various blogs. 

To help customers onboard easily with Content Agent, the UI now offers automatic destination creation to enable integration to SAP BTP services.

From security aspect this feature helps customer and partners to regularly rotate service instance credentials saved in the specific destinations. 

Procedure to Activate Content Type

To enable the content type click on Activate Content Type in Content Type page of the Content Agent UI and select the scenario for which you want to transport the content. Follow the step by step wizard based approach. 
In this procedure the user is asked to authenticate CF login URL by using SAML based SSO authentication. Once authenticated, in the next step the environment details for org and space in which the service instance and service key should be created. The org is already pre selected. After reviewing the providing input as the user clicks on next step, the procedure starts with creating destination.

 

Procedure to create TransportManagementService destination

In order to transport from the source account, you need to create a destination pointing to Cloud Transport Management service. The UI offers a wizard based step by step solution to achieve the same. Start the process by clicking on Create Destination.
The user needs to perform Authentication to CF Login Endpoint and select the CF environment details where the service instance of Cloud Transport service should be created (Central Service Administrator subaccount).  In the next step you have to provide a name with “TransportManagementService” as prefix. After reviewing the provided input, Content Agent will create the desired destination. 
Note: Entitlements are required for "standard" service plan of SAP Cloud Transport Management service. 

Thanks and enjoy reading!
Gauravi

entry.title: Re: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide

entry.title: Re: How to add Odata Service to a Fiori App?

entry.title: Re: Consuming a Third-Party SOAP Web Service in SAP Public Cloud via RAP
entry.content: Hi @Enda,

entry.title: Re: Z migration object names generated for Z projects in...
entry.content:
The main reason for Z migration objects is the following:
SAP delivers standard data migration objects within a standard delivery migration project.

A user creates an own migration project via the migration cockpit and this project is created in customer namespace starting with Z.
Such projects are having the technical name ZSIN_MIG_<mass transfer ID>
For example ZSIN_MIG_NBL.

Adding migration objects to that project, the migration cockpit's framework copies the original SAP migration object (for example, SIF_CUSTOMER_2) into the user's migration project.
The technical name of the copied migration object is as follows:  Z_<ALIAS>_<mass transfer id> 
For example, Z_CUSTOMER_2_NBL
<ALIAS> is the technical migration object name without prefix SIF_
<mass transfer id> is three letter id assigned to a migration project during its creation.

User defined data migration object, also known as custom migration objects, are having prefix Z_Z_

A user always works on a copy of an original object in customer namespace and not on the original object.

entry.title: On Capture of Invoice the Goods Receipt is to be Generated Automatically
entry.content:
S4Hana Public Cloud - Is the following supported in Public cloud and if so how?

We would like to automate the Goods Receipt process, on receipt and capture of the Invoice, for Invoices lower than a certain value.

For example if we had a PO for £200 and we received the Invoice for £200. We then capture the invoice and the Goods Receipt is automatically processed. This would be for non essential goods procurement.

Any guidance will be gratefully received.

Many thanks.

entry.title: Re: Premium Hub CoE – DSC Knowledge Bits Series – Field Catalogs for Transportation Cockpit in SAP T
entry.content:
Dear @SureshK2 @princeagarwal35 ,

Thank you for your questions! Please, check SAP Note 3562800 - TC: Not possible to set a customer field as an input field - Coding corrections - SAP for Me. Once SAP Note is implemented, you can make the field catalogue field editable in a post-exit of the method /SCMTMS/CL_UI_PLN_UIBB_BASE->GET_ENHANCEMENT_FIELD_DESCR.

Best regards,

Aleksey

entry.title: Re: Consuming a Third-Party SOAP Web Service in SAP Publi...
entry.content:
Hi @Enda 

Thank you very much for your quick response.

I solved the problem, it was my mistake.

I was getting the following error.

SoapFaulCodeName: ActionNotSupported |SoapFaulCodeNamespace: http://schemas.microsoft.com/ws/2005/05/addressing/none

This was because I did not add soap action to destination.

Old Code:

TRY.
DATA(destination) = cl_soap_destination_provider=>create_by_url( i_url = 'XXXXXXX' ).

DATA(proxy) = NEW zco_iaccount_statement_service( destination = destination ).

" fill request 
DATA(request) = VALUE ziaccount_statement_service_g1( ). 

request-request-base-ext_uname = 'XXXXX'. 
request-request-base-ext_upassword = 'XXXXX'. 

proxy->get_account_statement( 
EXPORTING 
input = request 
IMPORTING 
output = DATA(response) 
). 

CATCH cx_soap_destination_error INTO DATA(lo_err). 
out->write( lo_err->get_longtext( ) ). 

CATCH cx_ai_system_fault INTO DATA(lo_err2). 
out->write( lo_err2->get_longtext( ) ). 

ENDTRY

 

Corrected Code:

TRY. 
DATA(destination) = cl_soap_destination_provider=>create_by_url( i_url = 'XXXXXXX' ). 


destination->set_soap_action( 
EXPORTING 
i_operation = 'XXXXXX' 
i_action = 'XXXXXXXX' 

). 

DATA(proxy) = NEW zco_iaccount_statement_service( destination = destination ). 

" fill request 
DATA(request) = VALUE ziaccount_statement_service_g1( ). 

request-request-base-ext_uname = 'XXXXX'. 
request-request-base-ext_upassword = 'XXXXX'. 

proxy->get_account_statement( 
EXPORTING 
input = request 
IMPORTING 
output = DATA(response) 
). 

CATCH cx_soap_destination_error INTO DATA(lo_err). 
out->write( lo_err->get_longtext( ) ). 

CATCH cx_ai_system_fault INTO DATA(lo_err2). 
out->write( lo_err2->get_longtext( ) ). 

ENDTRY.

entry.title: SAP Signavio Language Layer
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
We have an issue with saving the second language layer in our dictionary items, this works on diagram level, but not at dictionary level. Please assist in fixing the problem:

Issue with language on dictionary items:

Example of a Role

Change Language to German and update:

Save and Yes

Select and Edit the role again

English open as saved

German values did not save

@Signavio @SignavioUser  @Mattsignavio @Su_Signavio @Signavio_1 

SAP Signavio Process Manager  SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub

entry.title: Do we have an option in Public cloud to schedule a report and send to specific mail id
entry.content: Business is planning to use the option for all possible reports like GL line-item report, Supplier line items, customer line items, etc. Do we have an option in Public cloud to schedule a report and send to specific mail id

entry.title: Re: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide
entry.content:
Hi @Gbaptista7 ,

You may try checking the following things:

1.Check the blog:common issues faced in Joule activation for S/4 HANA Private Cloud system. 

2. Ensure the principal propagation is working fine. If so, check the monitoring in cloud connector. 

3. Ensure the APIs mentioned in the SAP Note 3523238 ( Attachment -> Business Roles and API for Transactional Capabilities.xlsx) are active in the S/4 HANA system. Also, ensure the business roles mentioned in the attachments in the given note are assigned to the user. 

4. Make sure the role provisioning job runs post assignment of the roles. Check the roles are moved to SAP Build workzone by going to the settings->Identity provisioning (Search roles with the email id). 

 

Hope the above steps can help in troubleshooting.

entry.title: Re: In BusinessObjects the system administrator need thei...
entry.content: I don't know that the Administrator "has to be" configured as a Named User, but it certainly makes sense that it should be a Named User.  If the Administrator account is configured as a Named User, it will consume a license.

entry.title: Re: Is there a solution to prevent personal email on from being visible in Spotlight?
entry.content: Hello, We are in the midst of implementing Onboarding (2.0) migrating from 1.0. This topic is of great concern and how to work around this. The personal email used in Onboarding is syncing to EP as it is set as Primary (the only email). See KB3149668 - Personal email address is synced to EP. How does SAP suggest we work around the visibility of the Email (a personal email) in Spotlight? Do we need to remove all visibility to any email? Is this possible? If any other customers already on ONB 2.0 have any insight that would be greatly appreciated. We have some employees that do not get a Business Email and so this is where the issue and concern are coming from.

entry.title: Re: HTTP 500 or Odata API response issue for ERP to Employee Central Confirmation
entry.content: Same issue here, there's some solution?

entry.title: Re: Payment Block
entry.content:
Thank you for the explanation.

It would be worth writing a few more words about the R lock. It is not displayed in the help text for the payment block in vendor master data, but it is mentioned in the help text for the BSEG-ZLSRP field:

'If invoices are to be blocked automatically due to differences in the invoice items, the system enters the payment block R in the vendor line of the accounting document. The field in the header of the invoice document remains empty.'

entry.title: Re: Field Original Asset is not ready for input
entry.content:
There is an obsolete hyperlink "Fixed Assets: Available Fields per Asset Class" within KBA 2883430 that pointed to a non-existing page on the Help Portal.

That information was moved from the separate help portal page into the migration objects documentation of the fixed asset object(s).

You can find the information per release within section "Available Fields per Asset Class"

The subsection "Screen Layout Mapping" shows the the Screen Layout names and the subsection "Field Properties" lists the visibilty status per field in the screen layouts.
Dependent on your Screen Layout a field can be defined Invisible, optional, read only or mandatory. Invisible and read only fields cannot be migrated.

entry.title: Re: VFX3 Job Scheduling for Billing Release SAP
entry.content: Thank you!

entry.title: Re: What are your needs when importing? (And how you can best serve them)
entry.content: Very nice, very useful, very complete overview! Thanks, Karin!

entry.title: Re: Error Execution was halted due to an attempt to load...
entry.content: There is a solution for this issue in BI4.3 SP3 Patch 11 , although this is a product defect declared by SAP.

entry.title: Overhead getting calculated multiple times when using Origin groups in Costing sheet
entry.content:
We are Planning to use Origin Groups in Material master for Tariff on Materials imported.

We have created origin Groups for 10 countries for testing and assigned to Origin groups to imported raw materials.

We have also created new Calculation Base in our costing sheet with cost element group and Origin group as below:

For this calculation base,  we have assigned a OH rate. 

When we run costing run (CK11N) for Raw material A  the OH rate for tariff is coming correctly and overhead amount is calculated correctly. But when we do costing run (CK11N) for Finished product using Raw material A then SAP again shows the tariff overhead on the Raw material A . So the Tariff overhead is getting calculated TWICE. 

Please confirm how we can avoid this Duplicate calculation.

entry.title: Calling All Applicants for the 2025 Premium Hub CoE High School Summer Orientation!
entry.content:
Each year, SAP’s Premium Hub Center of Expertise (CoE) in North America hosts high school students to participate in a weeklong Summer Orientation. The 25-hour program provides students with the opportunity to learn about SAP technologies through different activities while receiving educational and career guidance from professionals in the field. SAP experts lead daily, interactive sessions that cover a variety of exciting topics, from coding and data analytics to SAP Cloud, IoT, and Mobile platforms. Over the course of the week, students use their technical skills and newly acquired knowledge to resolve real-world scenarios using SAP technologies. This year we are excited to host the orientation in-person at the Newtown Square campus (outside of Philadelphia). There, students will be able to explore the office, as well as participate in hands-on activities.   

Every year the Summer Orientation features a jam-packed schedule, from expert innovation sessions to career development guidance. The week starts off with a brief introduction of SAP along with a presentation on the various internship opportunities offered to college students. Students also have a career tip session, where they receive professional advice about their educational journey and beyond. Later in the week, students get to meet Balaji Gaddam, Head of Premium Hub CoE North America.

Students work on a primary project throughout the week that is always relevant to the latest in technology. The students’ work has evolved over the years, much like how SAP’s products have evolved as well! In previous years of the program, students have built projects using a wide variety of SAP technologies. At the end of the week, students will be able to showcase their project to their fellow peers and Premium Hub CoE team members.  

High school students interested in attending the 2025 Premium Hub CoE Summer Orientation should fill out the form below and update their most recent transcript. Space is limited for this competitive program and is geared towards rising Juniors and Seniors. Transcripts are required to be considered for the program so they must be uploaded to be considered. No resumes or cover letters are needed. Ideal candidates will have a strong interest in STEM and a desire to pursue further study and/or a career in a STEM-related field.  

Apply to the STEM Week here.  

Applications will be accepted until Friday, June 13. Students accepted to the program will be notified of the decision by Friday, June 27. The orientation will run in-person at the SAP Newtown Square Campus from July 21 to July 25.  The general hours are 10 am to 3 pm but early dismissal on Friday. Transportation will need to be arranged for the student as we do not provide this. Please note that this is not an internship, it is an unpaid program, and all costs associated with travel will need to be covered by the student. SAP will not cover any costs.

entry.title: Re: How to export data to excel by code?
entry.content: The big question: why do you need to change the way the dates are formatted by CL_SALV_TABLE=>TO_XML? If you want to do special formatting, as already explained in the forum many times, use abap2xlsx or XCO.

entry.title: Re: Industry Field Not Updated in supplier invoice line items
entry.content: On which version : On premise, private cloud, public cloud ?

entry.title: Re: Integration Center demo for the Compensation Module
entry.content: @SuryaRam Not possible if your compensation form isn't integrated with EC. You will have to keep doing manual upload and when the compensation template is integrated to EC then you can design a workaround with MDF.

entry.title: Confirm parent order when child order is confirmed
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement where top level should be a phantom assembly and its next level to be inventoried, not the top level.

So is there any way that child order can confirmed and then parent will be auto confirmed, but inventory should show for child, not parent.

Any suggestion please.

Thank you.

Regards,

John S.

entry.title: Re: How to export data to excel by code?
entry.content:
Also, please use the code formatting button as explained here: Solved: How to post code in SAP Community >=2024 - SAP Community

REPORT zzsro_test.
FORM download_data_to_excel USING p_table TYPE tabname.
  DATA lt_fields    TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF dfies.
  DATA lt_data      TYPE REF TO data.
  DATA lv_localpath TYPE string.
  DATA lv_msg       TYPE string.
  DATA lo_alv       TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table.
  DATA lv_xstring   TYPE xstring.
  DATA lv_bytecount TYPE i.
  DATA lt_solix     TYPE solix_tab.

  FIELD-SYMBOLS <lt_table> TYPE STANDARD TABLE.

  " TODO: variable is never used (ABAP cleaner)
  DATA ls_field TYPE dfies.

  " Lấy metadata bảng
  CALL FUNCTION 'DDIF_FIELDINFO_GET'
    EXPORTING  tabname   = p_table
               langu     = sy-langu
    TABLES     dfies_tab = lt_fields
    EXCEPTIONS not_found = 1
               OTHERS    = 2.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0 OR lt_fields IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE ':cross_mark: Không thể lấy metadata của bảng.' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

  " Tạo bảng động chứa dữ liệu
  CREATE DATA lt_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF (p_table).
  ASSIGN lt_data->* TO <lt_table>.

  " Lấy toàn bộ dữ liệu từ bảng
  SELECT * FROM (p_table) INTO TABLE @<lt_table>.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0 OR lines( <lt_table> ) = 0.
    MESSAGE ':information: Bảng không có dữ liệu để xuất.' TYPE 'I'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  " Hộp thoại chọn nơi lưu file Excel
  cl_gui_frontend_services=>file_save_dialog( EXPORTING  default_extension   = 'xlsx'
                                                         default_file_name   = |Data_{ p_table }_{ sy-datum }_{ sy-uzeit }.xlsx|
                                                         initial_directory   = 'C:\TEMP\'
                                                         prompt_on_overwrite = 'X'
                                              CHANGING   filename            = lv_localpath
                                                         path                = lv_msg
                                                         fullpath            = lv_localpath
                                              EXCEPTIONS cntl_error          = 1
                                                         error_no_gui        = 2
                                                         OTHERS              = 3 ).
  IF sy-subrc <> 0 OR lv_localpath IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE ':information: Bạn chưa chọn nơi lưu.' TYPE 'I'.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  " Tạo ALV từ dữ liệu
  TRY.
      cl_salv_table=>factory( IMPORTING r_salv_table = lo_alv
                              CHANGING  t_table      = <lt_table> ).

      " Đặt tiêu đề cột đơn giản
      DATA(lo_columns) = lo_alv->get_columns( ).
      lo_columns->set_optimize( abap_true ).

      " Chuyển đổi sang XLSX
      lv_xstring = lo_alv->to_xml( xml_type = if_salv_bs_xml=>c_type_xlsx ).

      " Chuyển XSTRING sang SOLIX
      CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY'
        EXPORTING buffer        = lv_xstring
        IMPORTING output_length = lv_bytecount
        TABLES    binary_tab    = lt_solix.

      " Tải file xuống
      cl_gui_frontend_services=>gui_download( EXPORTING  bin_filesize            = lv_bytecount
                                                         filename                = lv_localpath
                                                         filetype                = 'BIN'
                                              CHANGING   data_tab                = lt_solix
                                              EXCEPTIONS file_write_error        = 1
                                                         no_batch                = 2
                                                         gui_refuse_filetransfer = 3
                                                         invalid_type            = 4
                                                         no_authority            = 5
                                                         unknown_error           = 6
                                                         header_not_allowed      = 7
                                                         separator_not_allowed   = 8
                                                         filesize_not_allowed    = 9
                                                         header_too_long         = 10
                                                         dp_error_create         = 11
                                                         dp_error_send           = 12
                                                         dp_error_write          = 13
                                                         unknown_dp_error        = 14
                                                         access_denied           = 15
                                                         dp_out_of_memory        = 16
                                                         disk_full               = 17
                                                         dp_timeout              = 18
                                                         file_not_found          = 19
                                                         dataprovider_exception  = 20
                                                         control_flush_error     = 21
                                                         not_supported_by_gui    = 22
                                                         error_no_gui            = 23
                                                         OTHERS                  = 24 ).
      IF sy-subrc <> 0.
        MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
                WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
      ELSE.
        MESSAGE |:white_heavy_check_mark: Xuất dữ liệu thành công. Tổng cộng { lines( <lt_table> ) } dòng.| TYPE 'S'.
      ENDIF.

    CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_error).
      MESSAGE |:cross_mark: Lỗi khi xuất file Excel: { lx_error->get_text( ) }| TYPE 'E'.
  ENDTRY.
ENDFORM.

LOOP AT lt_fields INTO ls_field.
  ASSIGN COMPONENT ls_field-fieldname OF STRUCTURE <ls_row> TO <lv_value>.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0 OR <lv_value> IS INITIAL.
    CONTINUE.
  ENDIF.

  CALL METHOD OF lo_sheet 'Cells' = lo_cell
    EXPORTING #1 = lv_row
              #2 = lv_col.

  IF ls_field-datatype = 'DATS'.
    CALL FUNCTION convert DATE TO INTERNAL
      EXPORTING date_external            = <lv_value>
                accept_initial_date      = abap_on
      importing
                date_internal            = lv_data
      exceptions
                date_external_is_invalid = 1
                others                   = 2.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      " Implement suitable error handling here
    ENDIF.
    SET PROPERTY OF lo_cell 'Value' = lv_data.
  ELSEIF ls_field-datatype = 'TIMS'.

    WRITE <lv_value> TO lv_data2.
    REPLACE ALL OCCURRENCES OF ':' IN lv_data2 WITH ' '.
    SET PROPERTY OF lo_cell 'Value' = lv_data2.
  ELSE.

    SET PROPERTY OF lo_cell 'Value' = <lv_value>. " 20250610
    SET PROPERTY OF lo cell 'NumberFormat' = '@'.
  ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

entry.title: Re: Settlement Management- Contracts where Settlement Date does not align with Rebate Condition vali
entry.content: Hi, I am facing same issue. Are you able to fix this?

entry.title: Re: How to Use Standard SAP Tables (BKPF, BSEG) in CDS Vi...
entry.content:
Only SAP can release SAP objects.

Some interesting information in this blog post (e.g. released CDS views for BKPF/BSEG/ACDOCA): Smooth transition to ABAP for Cloud Development(Ch... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Deploying App into SAP Build Work Zone / Fiori Launchpad in BTP
entry.content:
@kranthinbv 

You do not need to create multiple sites to point to different systems. Work Zone main goal is to serve as a central launchpad.

If you mean a custom app pointing to multiple back end systems, just create an OData service on each of them. Those will be multiple and separate models inside your app. In BTP you will use multiple destinations. As simple as that.

If you mean exposing apps available in multiple back end systems to Work Zone, this is also possible. Basically you can achieve that by creating multiple content channels. 

This might be a good topic for a separate question in the Community.
